summary: Linked/matched three item translation units, itlipstickspore, itpikachutjoltair, and itlikelike, by promoting data tables into C, tightening function prototypes, adjusting symbol scope/local labels, and applying small code-shape fixes. The PR also improved ithitodeman data and flipped ithitodeman to Matching in configure.py, but the author explicitly noted hitodeman was not fully solved and might need to be resplit. decomp.dev reported +15984 linked code bytes, +2360 matched code bytes, +688 linked data bytes, +160 matched data bytes, and 9 new matches.
what_changed: Changed configure.py entries from NonMatching to Matching for melee/it/items/itlipstickspore.c, itpikachutjoltair.c, ithitodeman.c, and itlikelike.c.
Added item state table data in C, including it_803F6680 for lipstick spore, it_803F71D8 for Pikachu thunder jolt air, and it_803F8128/it_803F8180 for hitodeman-related data.

Adjusted config/GALE01/symbols.txt to mark small callback functions such as itLipstickspore_UnkMotion1_Coll and itPikachutjoltair_UnkMotion0_Coll as local, and converted many unnamed data/string/float symbols from global it_80xxxxxx names to local @NNN labels.
Renamed the rodata symbol at 0x803B86D8 from it_803B86D8 to zero_vec with size 0xC, enabling direct Vec3 initialization in itlikelike.

Corrected it_8029A114's signature from (Fighter_GObj*, Vec3*, int, f32) to (Fighter_GObj*, Vec3*, f32 facing_dir, s32 arg4), updated itlipstickspore.h, and fixed the itlipstick.c caller argument order.
Gave ftCo_800C78B0 a concrete prototype, UNK_RET ftCo_800C78B0(HSD_GObj* gobj, HSD_GObj* owner), in both ft_0C31.c and ft_0C31.h.
Gave ithitodeman's it_802D43AC a concrete Item_GObj* parameter and removed an unnecessary function-pointer cast at the it_8027A118 callsite.
Reworked itpikachutjoltair code shape with GET_ITEM/GET_JOBJ usage, NULL returns, smaller PAD_STACK adjustments, moved the trivial collision callback definition, and reduced unnecessary casts.

Reworked itlikelike matching hotspots around dropped/physics/spawn handling, including an inline helper it_802DC4BC_ptr, explicit itECB stack temporaries, Vec3 sp50 = zero_vec initialization, and PAD_STACK usage.
Bot report showed data sections for itlipstickspore and itpikachutjoltair reached 100%, itlikelike rodata reached 100%, and several one-byte code mismatches were resolved.
smart_moves: Resolved linkability by moving item state tables into source instead of leaving them as anonymous external data, which directly improved linked data coverage.
Used symbol scope cleanup aggressively: tiny static callbacks and compiler-generated literals were made local in symbols.txt, avoiding accidental global symbol requirements.
Fixed a real API mismatch for it_8029A114 by aligning the header, definition, and caller argument order; this removed ambiguity between facing_dir and the integer item variable.
For itlikelike, introduced a static inline helper and duplicated/reordered operations only where needed to satisfy code generation, while preserving existing behavior.
Used explicit stack padding and local temporaries such as itECB ecb and Vec3 sp50 to control stack layout and register allocation in known-sensitive matching functions.
Converted placeholder/UNK prototypes to typed HSD_GObj* or Item_GObj* signatures where callsites exposed the true types, reducing casts and improving future matching leverage.
decomp_lessons: When linking an item object, data tables are often the blocker as much as text: define ItemStateTable arrays in C and ensure symbol scope matches whether callbacks are static.
Small 0x8 collision callbacks that just return false can still affect link state; making them static/local can be required when their only references are through local state tables.
Argument order in headers matters for matching even when types are compatible enough to compile; verify callers and callee together before permuting code.
zero_vec-style shared constants should be named only when the code genuinely uses them as shared data; otherwise generated literals and strings should remain local @ labels.
For item spawn functions, direct initialization such as Vec3 sp50 = zero_vec can fix data references and stack layout simultaneously.

Partial progress on a stubborn unit can be worthwhile, but author notes matter: hitodeman improved and was marked Matching in configure.py, yet the PR body says hitodeman was not fully figured out and may need resplitting.
Using GET_ITEM and GET_JOBJ macros instead of raw user_data/hsd_obj access can improve source clarity and sometimes helps codegen alignment.
Do not overinterpret one-byte bot matches; in this PR several +1 byte improvements accompanied larger data/linking fixes, but the main win was linking whole item objects.
naming_conventions: Item state table symbols retained address-style names when no semantic name was known, e.g. it_803F6680, it_803F71D8, it_803F8128, and it_803F8180.
Compiler-generated local literals and constants were represented with @NNN labels in symbols.txt rather than global it_80xxxxxx names.
The project uses both itLikelike_* for motion callbacks and itLikeLike_Logic5_* for logic callbacks in the same item file; preserve existing capitalization rather than normalizing it.
Lipstick spore symbols appear as itLipstickspore_* for motion callbacks and itLipstickSpore_Logic37_* for logic callbacks; follow existing symbol spelling.
Named shared vector data as zero_vec when used as a Vec3 zero initializer instead of keeping the raw address name it_803B86D8.
Typed parameters were named for meaning when known, e.g. facing_dir and owner, while unknown integer behavior stayed generic as arg4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK calls with specific sizes in sensitive functions to reproduce stack layout.
Introduced static inline helpers, notably it_802DC4BC_ptr, to control inlining and call-shape in itlikelike.
Duplicated a block in it_2725_Logic5_Dropped with a comment 'permuterslop', suggesting source was shaped to satisfy compiler permutation/register allocation rather than pure refactoring.
Used direct local initialization Vec3 sp50 = zero_vec to match a spawn helper's expected data reference.
Removed unnecessary casts from item/fighter object calls once prototypes were corrected, e.g. Item_80268E5C(gobj, ...) and it_802B3544(ip->...xDD8).
Changed return constants from 0 to NULL in pointer-returning code to better match typed C source.
Moved the false-return collision callback in itpikachutjoltair after its use through the state table while keeping a forward static prototype.
Adjusted math expression casting in itpikachutjoltair, e.g. HSD_JObjSetRotationY(jobj, (M_PI_2 * ip->facing_dir)), reducing explicit f64/f32 cast noise.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The PR body states: 'I tried linking hitodman as well, but I couldn't figure out how. Might need to be resplit?'

decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 improvements: matched code 63.39% (+0.06%, +2360 bytes), linked code 34.83% (+0.41%, +15984 bytes), matched data 38.16% (+0.01%, +160 bytes), linked data 32.50% (+0.06%, +688 bytes).
decomp.dev listed 9 new matches, including full data matches for itlipstickspore and itpikachutjoltair, itlikelike rodata reaching 100%, and partial ithitodeman .data improvement to 63.37%.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for hitodeman resplitting or completion after the author noted it might need to be resplit.
Search for additional it_8029A114 callers to ensure all use the corrected (facing_dir, arg4) argument order.
Look for subsequent cleanups that replace address-style item state table names with semantic names.
Compare itlikelike matching tactics in later PRs to see whether the 'permuterslop' duplicated block was simplified after more context was decompiled.
